new_classes.label_en: Still Fermented Beverage from Concentrated Grape Must in containers > 2 L (CN 2206 00 89)
new_classes.label_de: Stilles gegorenes Getränk aus konzentriertem Traubenmost in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2206 00 89)
new_classes.definition_en: A fermented beverage that is not the product of the natural fermentation of must from fresh grapes, but is obtained from concentrated grape must; presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres; non-sparkling (still). Classified under CN 2206 00 89.

coverage_explanation: The two principal distinguishing criteria for CN 2206 00 89 are captured: (1) the beverage is produced by fermentation of concentrated grape must (not by natural fermentation of fresh grape must), modelled via the existential restriction on ConcentratedGrapeMustFermentationProcess; and (2) it is presented in containers with a volume greater than 2 litres, modelled via the existential restriction on LargeContainer (≥ 2 L per TBox definition — the CN legal boundary is > 2 L; the TBox LargeContainer uses ≥ 2 L which slightly overlaps the 2 L boundary shared with 2206 00 51, but this is the closest available modelling). The non-sparkling (still) character is modelled via a complement restriction excluding CarbonatedQuality. A small gap remains: the legal text notes that fermentation of the concentrated must is typically induced later (deferred fermentation), which is a process detail not fully capturable with the existing TBox process classes. The container threshold semantics (> 2 L vs ≥ 2 L) are also a minor imprecision given the existing LargeContainer definition.
